task_summary:
**Task:** A Standard customer is returning a defective blender from a free-shipping order. The defect means there is no restocking fee and the item itself stays on the full defective-return path, but the order still drops below the free-shipping threshold afterward, so the shipping clawback remains part of the overall resolution.

**Challenge:** The trap is assuming 'defective' wipes away every downstream consequence. Weak agents treat the case as globally deduction-free and never account for the separate order-level shipping effect.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent must apply shipping clawback even on defective returns.

opening_message: The blender from order ORD-7109 is defective — it won't turn on. I need a full refund.

user_simulator.user_sim_context: The blender is defective, so you expect the return itself to be treated fully in your favor. You are not thinking about the fact that returning it may still change the economics of the original order's shipping qualification. The agent should keep the product defect clear while also explaining any separate order-level consequence.
task_rules: Describe the defect: blender won't power on.
If any deductions mentioned, ask why (defective should be free).
Accept if agent explains shipping threshold policy.
